policy_name: Agreement of Lease — 498 Seventh Avenue, COSI, INC. (August 16, 2002)

clauses.description: Landlord leases to Tenant the Premises for a term commencing on the Commencement Date and ending on the last day of the calendar month which is three and one-half (3½) months after the tenth (10th) anniversary of the Commencement Date (the 'Expiration Date'), unless sooner terminated.

Tenant accepts the Premises in 'AS IS' condition on the Commencement Date, subject to latent defects. Landlord has no obligation to perform work or make alterations except as expressly set forth in the Lease and Schedule A (Landlord's Work). Tenant must comply with the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 and environmental laws pertaining to hazardous materials.

Landlord shall provide Tenant with ten (10) days written notice of the date the Premises will be ready for occupancy. The Commencement Date is the date of delivery of possession of the Premises to Tenant, free and clear of occupants, with Landlord's Work Substantially Complete.

Landlord agrees to advise Tenant of the anticipated cost of the unisex lavatory. Tenant has the option, exercisable within three (3) business days of receipt of the cost, to receive a rent credit equal to the cost amount, in which case Landlord is relieved of all responsibility for the lavatory.

Annual Fixed Rent during the Term shall be: (a) $225,000.00 per annum ($18,750.00/month) from Rent Commencement Date through the day before the 3rd anniversary; (b) $247,500.00 per annum ($20,625.00/month) from the 3rd through the 6th anniversary; (c) $272,250.00 per annum ($22,687.50/month) from the 6th anniversary through the Expiration Date. Fixed Rent is payable monthly in advance on the 1st of each month without set-off or deduction.

If the Rent Commencement Date occurs other than on the first day of a calendar month, Tenant shall pay a prorated daily amount for the period from the Rent Commencement Date to the last day of that month, at the rate of $625.00 per day.

Provided Tenant is not in material default, Tenant shall receive a credit against Fixed Rent installments for months 5 through 22 in the aggregate amount of $56,250.00, applied in 18 equal installments of $3,125.00 per month. Additionally, Landlord agrees to reimburse Tenant $18,000.00 within 30 days after Tenant opens for business.

Tenant has the option to extend the Lease term for two (2) additional periods of five (5) years each (Renewal Terms). The first Renewal Term commences on the day after the Expiration Date; the second follows the first. Renewal Notice for the first Renewal Term must be given no earlier than 15 months and no later than 12 months before the Expiration Date. Time is of the essence for these notices.

If Tenant exercises the Renewal Option, the Renewal Term shall be on the same terms and conditions as this Lease except that (a) Fixed Rent shall be the Fair Market Rent, (b) Tenant shall not be entitled to any Fixed Rent credit, and (c) Tenant's right to renew shall not apply during the second Renewal Term.

The Fixed Rent for each Renewal Term shall equal the annual fair market rental value of the Premises as of the first day of the applicable Renewal Term, but in no case less than the total Rent payable by Tenant for the year immediately preceding the Renewal Term without abatement.

The Fair Market Rent shall be determined by Landlord on the basis of the highest and best use of the Premises assuming the Premises are free and clear of all leases and tenancies, except that the Fair Market Rent shall not be less than the total Rent payable by Tenant with respect to the year immediately preceding the Renewal Term in question without abatement.

Landlord shall give Tenant written notice (the 'Rent Notice') not later than nine (9) months prior to the Expiration Date which Rent Notice shall set forth Landlord's determination of the Fair Market Rent ('Landlord's Determination').

If Landlord's Determination exceeds the Fixed Rent payable by Tenant on the Expiration Date, Tenant shall give Landlord written notice ('Tenant's Notice') within forty (40) days after Tenant's receipt of the Rent Notice, of whether Tenant accepts or disputes Landlord's Determination. If Tenant disputes, Tenant's Notice shall contain Tenant's determination of the Fair Market Rent ('Tenant's Determination') and the name of an independent real estate appraiser ('Tenant's Consultant') to act as Tenant's representative.

Landlord shall give Tenant written notice ('Landlord's Notice') within twenty (20) days after Landlord's receipt of Tenant's Determination, as to whether Landlord accepts or disputes Tenant's Determination. If Landlord disputes, Landlord shall appoint an independent real estate appraiser ('Landlord's Consultant'). Not later than ten (10) Business Days after Tenant's receipt of Landlord's Notice, Tenant's Consultant and Landlord's Consultant shall meet to mutually agree upon the determination of the Fair Market Rent, which determination shall be final and binding upon the parties.

The Third Consultant shall conduct such hearings and investigations as he may deem appropriate and shall, within thirty (30) days after the date of designation of the Third Consultant, choose either Landlord's or Tenant's Determination, and such choice by the Third Consultant shall be conclusive and binding upon Landlord and Tenant. Each party shall pay its own counsel fees and expenses. Any Consultant appointed pursuant to this Paragraph D shall be an independent real estate broker with at least ten (10) years' experience in leasing of office space in commercial properties in midtown Manhattan which are similar in character to the Building.

After a determination has been made of the Rental Value for the applicable Renewal Term, the parties shall execute and deliver to each other an instrument setting forth the Rental Value for the Renewal Term in question as herein above determined.

If the final determination of the Rental Value shall not be made on or before the first day of each of the Renewal Terms, pending such final determination Tenant shall continue to pay, as the Fixed Rent for such Renewal Term, an amount equal to the Fixed Rent and all Additional Rent payable for the year immediately preceding the applicable Renewal Term without abatement. If, based upon the final determination hereunder, the payments made by Tenant on account of the Fixed Rent for such portion of the applicable Renewal Term were less than the Rental Value payable for the applicable Renewal Term, Tenant shall pay to Landlord the amount of such deficiency within ten (10) days after demand therefor.

If any Fixed Rent or Additional Rent becomes uncollectible by virtue of any Laws, Tenant shall enter into such agreements and take actions as Landlord may reasonably request to permit Landlord to collect the maximum Rent legally permissible during the restriction, without exceeding amounts due hereunder.

Tenant shall use and occupy the Premises as a first-class, high quality retail casual restaurant, including sale of salads, sandwiches, gourmet food, ice cream, frozen desserts, novelties, baked goods, alcoholic beverages (with license) for on-premises consumption only, and non-alcoholic beverages, and as a sit-down restaurant with table service at Tenant's option. Tenant shall operate under the trade name 'Cosi' or a successor name used by a majority of Tenant's first-class shops in New York City. Tenant shall obtain and maintain all necessary licenses and permits for the operation of its business in the Premises.

Tenant shall not use the Premises for: electronic data processing, banking, trust, savings, loan, pawnbroker, financial, securities, medical/dental/health services, drug store, governmental use, cooking other than gas oven baking of bread, pizza, and cooking pastas (frying or use of a fryolator is prohibited under any circumstances), employment agencies, schools, gambling, dance halls, pool halls, gymnasiums, live performances, fast food restaurants (including McDonalds, Burger King, KFC, Wendys, Arthur Treacher, White Castle), vending machines, bars, tobacco sales, magazine/book/video sales conspicuously visible from exterior, advertising, dry cleaning, discount store, funeral parlor, training or educational facility, food court, or any other prohibited use listed.

Tenant shall not use or permit the Premises to be used in any manner that would violate any Requirement, void or voidable any insurance policy, make fire insurance unobtainable at standard rates, cause injury or damage to the Real Property, constitute a public or private nuisance, emit objectionable noise, fumes, vapors or odors, impair Building services, or impair or interfere with the use of any other area of the Building.

Tenant shall not place a load upon any floor exceeding the designed floor load per square foot or any load allowed by any Requirement. Tenant shall not move heavy machinery, equipment, or fixtures into or out of the Building without Landlord's prior consent and shall pay Landlord's reasonable costs in connection therewith. If such machinery, equipment or fixtures require special handling, Tenant shall employ only persons holding a Master Rigger's license approved by Landlord.

Tenant shall not use or occupy the Premises in violation of the certificate of occupancy. Upon five (5) days' written notice from Landlord or any Governmental Authority, Tenant shall immediately discontinue any use that violates the certificate of occupancy.

Any vaults or vault space outside the boundaries of the Real Property are not included in the Premises and are used under a revocable license. Revocation or diminution of such license shall not constitute an eviction or entitle Tenant to any abatement of Rental.
Tenant acknowledges that the lobby newsstand of the Building sells items which Tenant will be selling, in addition to newspapers and magazines.

Definitions for Escalation Additional Rent purposes: 'Assessed Valuation' means the amount assessed under the NYC Charter; 'Base Taxes' means Taxes payable for the period July 1, 2002 through June 30, 2003; 'Taxes' means aggregate real estate taxes and assessments imposed on the Real Property; 'Tax Statement' means a statement comparing Taxes to Base Taxes and the Tax Payment due for the Tax Year; 'Tax Year' means July 1 through June 30; 'Tenant's Share' means 0.62%.

If Taxes for any Tax Year increase above Base Taxes, Tenant shall pay to Landlord as Escalation Additional Rent an amount equal to Tenant's Share (0.62%) of such increase in Taxes (the 'Tax Payment'), continuing until a new Tax Statement is rendered. Tenant is not entitled to any credit against Tax Payment for discounts, exemptions, or abatements received by Landlord.

Landlord shall render a Tax Statement to Tenant at any time during or after the Term. Tenant shall pay the Tax Payment shown thereon no later than 20 days after Landlord delivers the Tax Statement. Landlord's failure to render a Tax Statement for any Tax Year shall not prejudice Landlord's right to do so later.

The Tax Payment shall be pro rated for any partial Tax Year in which the Term commences or expires. If a Tax Statement is furnished after Landlord has paid a Tax installment for which Tenant is partly responsible, Tenant shall pay the amount of any underpayment within 20 days.

Only Landlord may institute tax reduction proceedings. If a refund of Taxes is received by Landlord after a Tax Statement has been sent to Tenant, Landlord shall send Tenant an adjusted Tax Statement and Tenant shall receive a credit for its share of the net refund, limited to amounts previously paid as Escalation Additional Rent for that Tax Year.

If after a Tax Statement has been sent to Tenant, the Assessed Valuation used to compute the Base Taxes is reduced, Base Taxes shall be retroactively adjusted to reflect such reduction, the prior installments of Escalation Additional Rent shall be increased accordingly, and all retroactive Escalation Additional Rent resulting from such retroactive adjustment shall be due and payable when billed by Landlord.

If at any time during the Term the Assessed Valuation shall be reduced due to a condemnation, taking or casualty, Base Taxes shall be reduced by a percentage equal to the percentage by which the Taxes payable in the Tax Year during which such reduction in Assessed Valuation shall occur shall have been reduced.

Under no circumstances shall the Fixed Rent payable under this Lease be less than the Fixed Rent set forth in Article 1. All Escalation Additional Rent shall be collectible by Landlord in the same manner as Fixed Rent.

Expiration or termination of this Lease during any Tax Year shall not affect the rights or obligations of the parties respecting Escalation Additional Rent payments for that Tax Year. Any payments due under a Tax Statement may be sent to Tenant subsequent to termination, and all such amounts shall be payable within 20 days after the statement is sent.

Landlord has been granted benefits under the Industrial and Commercial Incentive Program (ICIP). In connection therewith, all of Tenant's construction managers, contractors and subcontractors shall be contractually required to comply with New York City OLS requirements, including submission of a Construction Employment Report, attendance at a pre-construction conference, and adherence to ICIP Rules and Regulations, NYC Charter Chapter 13-B, and Executive Order No. 50 (1980). At Landlord's request, Tenant shall (i) report the number of workers permanently engaged in employment in the premises and the nature of each worker's employment and, to the extent applicable, the New York City residency of each worker, (ii) provide access to the premises to employees and agents of the Department, and (iii) enforce the contractual obligations of Tenant's construction managers, contractors and subcontractors to comply with the OLS requirements.
Electric current shall be supplied to Tenant at the Premises in accordance with and subject to the provisions of Article 4.

Landlord has installed a submeter at Landlord's sole cost. Tenant shall pay for electricity at the charges, taxes, terms and rates actually charged to Landlord plus 7% to cover Landlord's administrative costs. The amounts on the Submeter shall be binding and conclusive on Tenant and shall be deemed additional rent without set-off or deduction.

Landlord may elect at any time to discontinue supplying electric current via submetering. If Landlord discontinues, Tenant shall arrange to obtain electric current directly from the utility corporation. Landlord shall not discontinue furnishing electric current until Tenant is able to obtain same directly from the utility corporation servicing the Building of which the Premises are a part.

Tenant's use of electric current shall not exceed the capacity of the electrical equipment and wiring in the Premises. Tenant shall not, without Landlord's prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld), make any alteration or addition to the electric system of the Premises. Should Landlord grant such consent, all additional risers or other equipment required therefor shall be provided by Landlord, and all costs and expenses in connection therewith, including those for filing and supervision, shall be paid by Tenant upon Landlord's demand, as additional rent, without set-off or deduction.

If any taxes are imposed upon Landlord in connection with the sale or resale of electrical energy, Tenant shall pay its pro rata share of such taxes upon demand as additional rent without set-off or deduction. Tenant shall comply with all General Rules, Regulations, Terms and Conditions applicable to service equipment, wiring and Requirements of the utility corporation supplying electric current to the Building.

At Landlord's option, and provided that the cost is commercially competitive, Tenant agrees to purchase from Landlord all lighting tubes, lamps, bulbs and ballasts used in the Premises and pay installation costs as additional rent within 20 days after rendition of bills therefor.

Tenant shall make no electrical installations, alterations, additions or changes to electrical equipment or appliances without prior written consent of Landlord (not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed). Tenant's electrical requirements shall not exceed the capacity of existing feeders or risers. If additional risers are needed, they shall be installed by Landlord at Tenant's sole expense as additional rent within 20 days after rendition of a bill to Tenant therefor.

400 amps of electric service is currently available in the Premises. If Tenant requires additional amps to operate its normal business, Landlord shall make available up to 200 additional amps in the Building, but all costs to bring such additional amps to the Premises shall be borne solely by Tenant and shall be performed subject to and in compliance with all the terms and conditions of this Lease.

Tenant shall not make any Alterations without Landlord's prior written consent. Landlord agrees not to unreasonably withhold or delay consent to nonstructural Alterations proposed to adapt the Premises for permitted business purposes, provided such Alterations (a) are performed only by Landlord's general contractor or contractors/electricians/mechanics reasonably satisfactory to Landlord, (b) do not affect any part of the Building other than the Premises, (c) do not affect any service to be furnished by Landlord, (d) do not reduce the value or utility of the Building, and (e) will not result in a violation of or require an amendment to the certificate of occupancy. If Landlord fails to approve or disapprove an Alteration within 14 days of receipt of plans, such Alteration shall be deemed approved.

Prior to making any Alterations, Tenant shall (a) submit to Landlord detailed plans and specifications and obtain Landlord's approval, (b) obtain all permits, approvals and certificates from Governmental Authorities at Tenant's expense, and (c) furnish to Landlord duplicate original policies of worker's compensation, builder's all-risk, and commercial general liability insurance in amounts Landlord may reasonably require, naming Landlord and its agent and any Mortgagee as additional insureds. Upon completion of such Alteration, Tenant shall obtain certificates of final approval of such Alteration required by any Governmental Authority.

All Alterations shall be performed at Tenant's expense at times and in a manner as Landlord may reasonably designate. All furniture, furnishings, movable fixtures and partitions and Alterations made by Tenant at its own cost and expense prior to and during the Term shall remain Tenant's property and may be removed at Expiration Date at Tenant's option, provided Tenant repairs any damage caused by such removal. Landlord may require removal of furniture, furnishings, movable fixtures, partitions and Alterations upon not less than 10 days' notice prior to the Expiration Date or earlier end of the Term.

Any mechanic's lien filed against the Premises or the Real Property for work claimed to have been done for or materials claimed to have been furnished to Tenant shall be discharged by Tenant within 30 days by payment or filing the bond required by law. Tenant shall not employ any contractor, mechanic or laborer in the Premises if such employment would cause interference or conflict with other contractors, mechanics or laborers engaged by Landlord.

Tenant may make interior non-structural decorative alterations (a) such as improvements, additions and decorations including changing color schemes, installing new countertops, flooring, wall covering and modifying tenant fixture layout, without obtaining Landlord's consent but upon prior notice to Landlord. However, Tenant shall not make any alterations affecting structure, plumbing, electrical or mechanical systems of the Building without Landlord's prior written consent, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed.

The schematic plans attached as Exhibit C are approved subject to Landlord's review and approval of final plans and specifications including without limitation mechanicals. Ventilation from the Premises will be required to vent through 37th Street in the location specified by Landlord as shown on Exhibit D. Landlord agrees not to unreasonably withhold or delay (and will respond within two (2) business days of receipt) approval as to ventilation.

If Tenant applies for a building permit and is delayed solely as a result of a violation against the Building and notifies Landlord thereof, Tenant shall be entitled to a rent credit for each day after Landlord's receipt of such notice until the violation is removed or Tenant obtains a building permit or is able to work in the Premises, whichever shall first occur.

Landlord shall maintain and repair the Building Systems (other than Tenant's air conditioning system, which shall be maintained and replaced by Tenant at its sole cost and expense), the Building's structural elements and the public portions of the Building, both exterior and interior. Tenant, at its sole cost and expense, shall take good care of the Premises and the fixtures, equipment and any air conditioning system therein and make all nonstructural repairs thereto as and when needed to preserve them in good working order and condition, except for reasonable wear and tear, obsolescence and damage for which Tenant is not responsible pursuant to the provisions of Article 10 hereof. All damage or injury to the Premises or to the Building Systems or any other part of the Building, whether requiring structural or nonstructural repairs, caused by or resulting from carelessness, omission, neglect or improper conduct of Tenant, Tenant's servants, employees, invitees or licensees, shall be repaired at Tenant's sole cost and expense.

Tenant shall, at its sole cost and expense, comply with all sprinkler system Requirements applicable to the Premises. If the New York Board of Fire Underwriters or the New York Fire Insurance Rating Organization requires installation or modification of a sprinkler system by reason of Tenant's business or the location of Tenant's partitions or trade fixtures, Tenant shall promptly make such installations, changes, modifications or alterations at its own cost. Landlord shall be responsible for all repairs and maintenance of any plumbing lines outside the Premises serving the sprinkler system (unless such repairs and/or maintenance are necessitated as a result of an act of Tenant).

Tenant shall cause the Premises, store front, entrance door(s) and all glass surfaces and service entrance area to be cleaned at regular intervals (not less than weekly), shall keep the sidewalk and curb directly in front of the Premises clean and free of snow (and shall not at any time sweep any refuse, rubbish or dirt into the gutters or streets), and shall immediately replace any broken or cracked exterior glass in the Premises including a so-called 'bulls-eye' break in the glass. Any replacement glass shall be of the same kind and quality as is currently in the Premises.

Tenant, at its sole expense, shall comply with all Requirements applicable to the Premises and its use and occupancy, including the Americans with Disabilities Act and regulations thereunder, the facade and the door to the Premises. Tenant shall not do or permit to be done any act or thing upon the Premises which will invalidate or be in conflict with any insurance policies covering the Building and fixtures and property therein, and shall not use the Premises in a manner that increases the rate of fire insurance on the Building or property located therein over that in effect immediately prior to any such increase.

Tenant shall not be obligated to comply with any Requirements or insurance requirements which require any structural Alteration of the Premises or payment of additional fire insurance rates unless such structural Alteration or fire insurance rate increase (a) is necessitated by a condition which has been otherwise created by, or at the instance of, Tenant, including without limitation, any Alterations made by Tenant, (b) is attributable to the specific use or manner of use to which Tenant puts the Premises, (c) is required by reason of a breach of Tenant's obligations hereunder, or (d) is occasioned, in whole or in part, by any act, omission or negligence of Tenant.

Tenant may contest, at its sole cost and expense, the legality or applicability of any Requirement affecting the Premises, provided that (i) Landlord and any Indemnities shall not be subject to criminal or civil penalties, nor shall the Real Property be subject to being condemned or vacated, nor shall the certificate of occupancy be suspended or threatened to be suspended by reason of non-compliance during such contest. Before the contest commences, Tenant shall furnish Landlord a security bond in an amount at least equal to 120% of (x) the cost of compliance, (y) the criminal or civil penalties and interest, and (z) the amount of liability to independent third parties.

Tenant and Tenant's servants, contractors, employees, agents, visitors and licensees shall comply with the Rules and Regulations. If Tenant disputes the reasonableness of any additional Rule or Regulation adopted by Landlord, the dispute shall be submitted to arbitration per Article 26. Landlord shall not enforce any Rule or Regulation against Tenant which Landlord is not then enforcing against all similarly situated retail tenants in the Building. In the event of inconsistency between the Rules and Regulations and this Lease, the terms and conditions of this Lease shall prevail.

If any governmental license or permit is required for the proper and lawful conduct of Tenant's business in the Premises, Tenant shall procure and maintain such license or permit and submit the same to Landlord for inspection. Tenant shall comply with the terms and conditions of each such license and permit. In no event shall failure to procure or maintain such license or permit by Tenant affect Tenant's obligations hereunder.

If due to any Requirement or insurance policy applicable to the Building, Landlord deems alterations in the Premises reasonably necessary, Landlord shall have access to perform the same, provided that if such performance results in a permanent reduction in the usable area of the Premises (except to a de minimis extent), the Fixed Rent shall be reduced by the proportion which the area of the Premises so taken bears to the total area of the Premises immediately prior thereto. Landlord shall use reasonable efforts so that such alterations shall not materially adversely block access to the Premises or materially adversely impact the economic operation of Tenant's business at the Premises.

This Lease is subject and subordinate to the lien of each and every Superior Lease and Mortgage. This clause is self-operative and no further instrument of subordination shall be required. However, Tenant shall execute and deliver promptly any certificate, in recordable form, that Landlord may request in confirmation of such subordination within 20 days of request. Landlord shall use reasonable efforts to obtain non-disturbance protection in favor of Tenant from any existing Mortgagee or Lessor and any future Mortgagee or Lessor.

Tenant irrevocably waives any and all rights it may have in connection with any zoning lot merger or transfer of development rights with respect to the Real Property, including rights to be a party to, to contest, or to execute any Declaration of Restrictions as defined in Section 12-10 of the Zoning Resolution of New York City effective December 15, 1961, as amended, with respect to the Real Property, which would cause the Premises to be merged with or unmerged from any other zoning lot.

If any Mortgagee comes into possession of the Real Property or the Building prior to the expiration of the Term, Tenant agrees, at the election and demand of any owner of the Real Property or any Mortgagee, to attorn to any such owner or Mortgagee upon the then executory terms and conditions of this Lease for the remainder of the Term, provided such owner or Mortgagee supplies Tenant with a non-disturbance agreement and Tenant is not disturbed in its use and possession of the Premises under this Lease so long as Tenant is not in default hereunder beyond the applicable notice and cure period.

Within 20 days following Tenant's receipt of a request by Landlord or any Mortgagee, Tenant shall deliver to Landlord and/or Mortgagee a written estoppel certificate in form reasonably satisfactory to Landlord and/or Mortgagee (i) stating that the Lease is in full force and effect and has not been modified (or if modified, setting forth all modifications), (ii) setting forth the date to which the Fixed Rent, Escalation Additional Rent and all other charges hereunder have been paid and the amounts thereof, (iii) stating whether or not, to the best of Tenant's knowledge, Landlord is in default under this Lease, and if Landlord is in default, setting forth the specific nature of all such defaults, and (iv) as to any other matters reasonably requested by Landlord and/or such Mortgagee.

As long as any Mortgage shall exist, Tenant shall not seek to terminate this Lease by reason of any act or omission of Landlord until Tenant has given written notice of such act or omission to all Mortgagees at such addresses as shall have been furnished to Tenant by such Mortgages, and until a reasonable period of time shall have elapsed following the giving of such notice, during which period such Mortgagees shall have the right, but not the obligation, to remedy such act or omission.

Neither Landlord nor its agents shall be liable for any damage to property of Tenant or others entrusted to employees of the Building, nor for any injury or damage to persons or property, or interruption of Tenant's business, resulting from fire or other casualty; nor shall Landlord or its agents be liable for any latent defect in the Premises or in the Building.

If any windows of the Premises are temporarily or permanently closed or bricked-up if required by law or related to construction upon property adjacent to the Real Property, Landlord shall not be liable for any damage Tenant may sustain and Tenant shall not be entitled to any compensation therefor nor abatement of Fixed Rent obligations, nor shall the same release Tenant or constitute an eviction, except that if all or substantially all of the windows of the Premises shall be closed or bricked-up for more than 3 Business Days and Tenant closes its business for such period by reason thereof, Tenant shall be entitled to an abatement of Rental until such closing or brick-up is removed or Tenant is legally permitted to re-open its business.

Tenant shall immediately notify Landlord of any fire or accident in the Premises or in the Building, and of any other damage to or defect in the Premises, the Building (including any equipment located therein) and the Building Systems to the extent (a) the same are Landlord's property, or (b) Landlord has an obligation to repair the same hereunder.

Tenant shall obtain and keep in full force and effect a policy of commercial general liability and property damage insurance (written on an occurrence basis) with a broad form contractual liability endorsement and completed operations endorsement, under which Tenant is named as the insured and Landlord and any Mortgagees and managing agents are named as additional insureds. Minimum limits of liability shall be a combined single limit of not less than $5,000,000 for injury or death and damage to property per occurrence. Tenant shall also obtain 'all risk' insurance covering all of Tenant's personal property and Alterations for 100% of replacement value. All insurance required to be carried by Tenant pursuant to the terms of this Lease shall be primary and shall be effected under valid and enforceable policies issued by reputable and independent insurers permitted to do business in the State of New York, and rated in Best's Insurance Guide as having a general policyholder rating of 'A' and a financial rating of at least '13'.

On or prior to the Commencement Date, Tenant shall deliver to Landlord appropriate Acord 27 certificates of insurance required to be carried by Tenant pursuant to Article 9, with a contractual liability endorsement and including evidence of waivers of subrogation required pursuant to Paragraph D of Article 9. Evidence of each renewal or replacement of a policy shall be delivered to Tenant to Landlord at least 20 days prior to the expiration of such policy. The Landlord shall maintain liability insurance with respect to the Building in a commercially reasonable amount.

Both parties shall procure an appropriate waiver of subrogation clause in, or endorsement on, any fire or extended coverage insurance covering the Premises, the Building and personal property, fixtures and equipment, pursuant to which the insurance companies waive subrogation or consent to a waiver of right of recovery, and will not make any claim against or seek to recover from the other for any loss or damage to its property or the property of others resulting from fire or other hazards covered by such fire and extended coverage insurance.

If the Premises shall be damaged by fire or other casualty, Tenant shall give prompt notice thereof to Landlord, and Landlord shall commence, without unreasonable delay, to repair the damage at Landlord's expense to the condition existing as of the Commencement Date, exclusive of Tenant's Alterations. Commencing on the date of such damage and continuing until the earlier of (i) fifteen (15) days after such repairs shall be made or (ii) the date the Premises shall be open for the conduct of the permitted use, the Fixed Rent shall be reduced in the proportion which the unusable area bears to the total area of the Premises. Landlord shall have no obligation to repair any damage to, or to replace, any Alterations, fixtures, furniture, furnishings, equipment or other property or effects of Tenant.

If the Premises are substantially damaged or rendered substantially untenantable, and if Landlord shall decide not to restore the Premises (if it shall be Landlord's obligation to do so), or if the Building shall be so damaged by fire or other casualty that substantial alteration, demolition or reconstruction of the Building shall be required, and Landlord also terminates a majority of all the retail leases in the Building, Landlord may terminate this Lease by not later than 90 days following the damage, by giving Tenant written notice. In such event, the Term shall expire upon the 10th day after such notice is given.

If all or any part of Tenant's Alterations are damaged or destroyed by fire or other casualty, Tenant shall commence promptly, and with reasonable dispatch, after Landlord substantially completes its restoration of the Premises, to restore the same to substantially the same condition as existed immediately preceding the damage or destruction, which restoration shall be performed pursuant to the provisions of this Lease, including but not limited to, Article 5.

If the Premises shall be substantially damaged during the last year of the Term, Landlord or Tenant may elect by notice given within 30 days after the occurrence of such damage to terminate this Lease, and if either party makes such election, the Term shall expire upon the 30th day after notice of such election is given, and Tenant shall vacate the Premises and surrender the same to Landlord.

No penalty shall accrue for delays which may arise by reason of adjustment of fire insurance on the part of Landlord or Tenant, or for delay on account of 'labor troubles' or any other cause beyond Landlord's control.

Within 45 days after notice to Landlord of any damage, Landlord shall deliver to Tenant a statement prepared by a reputable independent contractor setting forth the reasonable time required to repair the damage to the Premises (excluding Tenant's Alterations) and to repair such other damage which substantially damaged the Building or rendered the Building substantially untenantable. If the estimated repair time exceeds 12 months from the date of such statement, Tenant may elect to terminate this Lease by notice to Landlord given not later than 10 days following receipt of such statement.

If the whole of the Real Property, the Building or the Premises shall be acquired or condemned for any public or quasi-public use or purpose, this Lease and the Term shall end as of the date of the vesting of title with the same effect as if such date were the Expiration Date. If only a part of the Real Property shall be so acquired or condemned, the Lease shall continue in force but the Fixed Rent and Tenant's Share shall be reduced in the proportion which the area of the part of the Premises so acquired or condemned bears to the total area of the Premises immediately prior to such acquisition or condemnation.

In the event of any acquisition or condemnation of all or any part of the Real Property, Landlord shall be entitled to receive the entire award. Tenant shall have no claim against Landlord or the condemning authority for the value of any unexpired portion of the Term and Tenant hereby expressly assigns to Landlord all of its rights to any such award. Nothing in this Paragraph B shall be deemed to prevent Tenant from making a separate claim in any condemnation proceedings for the value of the unamortized cost of leasehold improvements installed by and at the sole expense of Tenant included in such taking, and for any moving expenses.

If the whole or any part of the Premises shall be acquired or condemned temporarily during the Term for any public or quasi-public use or purpose, Tenant shall give prompt notice thereof to Landlord and the Term shall not be reduced or affected in any way and Tenant shall continue to pay in full all items of Rental payable by Tenant hereunder without reduction or abatement, and Tenant shall be entitled to receive for itself any award or payments for such use.

Except as expressly permitted herein, Tenant shall not assign its rights or delegate its duties under this Lease, mortgage or encumber its interest, or sublet or permit the Premises or any part thereof to be occupied or used by any person other than Tenant, without the prior consent of Landlord in each instance. Landlord agrees to give Tenant any notice of default by any assignee or sublessee and allow Tenant the same opportunity to cure such default as provided in this Lease for such assignee or sublessee.

Landlord shall not unreasonably withhold or delay its consent to an assignment or subletting of the whole or a part of the Premises for substantially the remainder of the Term, provided Tenant furnishes Landlord with the name and business address of the proposed subtenant or assignee, information with respect to the nature and character of the proposed subtenant's or assignee's business, current financial information with respect to net worth, credit and financial responsibility as are reasonably satisfactory to Landlord, and an executed counterpart of the sublease or assignment agreement together with a schedule in reasonable detail setting forth any Sublease Profit or Assignment Proceeds and any claimed deductions therefrom.

If Tenant's interest in this Lease is assigned or transferred in violation of the provisions of this Article 12, such assignment or transfer shall be void and of no force and effect against Landlord; provided, however, that (a) Tenant shall pay to Landlord any consideration payable by such assignee or transferee for any such assignment or transfer of this Lease in violation of this Article 12, and (b) Landlord may collect from the assignee or transferee an amount equal to the greater of the then Fixed Rent or the then going market rental rate for comparable space and a comparable term in the Building.

Notwithstanding Paragraph A of this Article, Tenant may, without Landlord's consent but upon not less than 10 days' notice to Landlord, assign its interest in this Lease (i) to any corporation which is a successor to Tenant by merger or consolidation, (ii) to a purchaser of not less than 5 of Tenant's stores in the City of New York or a purchaser of all or substantially all of Tenant's business and assets in the City of New York (provided such purchaser shall have also assumed substantially all of Tenant's liabilities), (iii) to a corporation or entity which shall control, be under the control of, or be under common control with Tenant (the term 'control' as used herein shall be deemed to mean ownership of more than 50% of the outstanding voting stock of a corporation, or other majority equity and control interest), (iv) to a joint venture for the operation of the business in the Premises so long as Tenant's joint venture partner does not participate in the management or operation of said business, or (v) to a franchisee or licensee pursuant to a bona fide franchise or license agreement covering at least 3 of the stores operated by Tenant in the City, County and State of New York.

Except as set forth in Paragraph C above and except for the sale of shares on the 'over-the-counter market' or through any recognized stock exchange, a transfer of a controlling interest in the shares of Tenant (if Tenant is a corporation or trust) or a transfer of a majority of the total interest in Tenant (if Tenant is a partnership or other entity) at any one time or over a period of time through a series of transfers shall be deemed an assignment of this Lease and shall be subject to all provisions of this Article 12, including without limitation, the requirement that Tenant obtain Landlord's prior consent thereto, if (i) the transfer is not made for a valid business purpose, or (ii) is made to circumvent the provisions and requirements of this Article 12, or (iii) the operation of the business at the Premises changes as a result of such transfer.

If Tenant assumes this Lease and proposes to assign it pursuant to the Bankruptcy Code to any person or entity who shall have made a bona fide offer, notice of such proposed assignment shall be given to Landlord no later than 20 days after receipt of such offer by Tenant, but no later than 10 days prior to the date Tenant shall make application to a court of competent jurisdiction for authority and approval to enter into such assignment and assumption. Such notice shall set forth (a) the name and address of such person, (b) all of the terms and conditions of such offer, and (c) adequate assurance of future performance by such person under the Lease.

If, at any time after Tenant may have assigned Tenant's interest in this Lease, this Lease shall be disaffirmed or rejected in any proceeding of the types described in subparagraph (v) of Paragraph A of Article 14 hereof, or in any similar proceeding, or in the event of termination of this Lease by reason of any such proceeding or by reason of lapse of time following notice of termination given pursuant to said Article 14 based upon any of the Events of Default set forth in said subparagraph (v), Tenant, upon request of Landlord given within thirty (30) days next following any such disaffirmance, rejection or termination, shall (a) pay to Landlord all Fixed Rent, Escalation Additional Rent and other items of Rental due and owing by the assignee to Landlord under this Lease to and including the date of such disaffirmance, rejection or termination, and (b) as 'tenant', enter into a new lease with Landlord of the Premises for a term commencing on the effective date of such disaffirmance, rejection or termination and ending on the Expiration Date.

If Landlord consents to any assignment or sublease to a third party (but not including any transfer under Paragraph C above), Tenant shall pay to Landlord as additional rent one-half (½) of any rent or other consideration paid by any subtenant or undertenant to Tenant or anyone claiming under or through Tenant in excess of the pro rata portion of the then Fixed Rent payable for such sublease space, plus one-half (½) of any other amount received by Tenant or anyone claiming under Tenant in connection with such subletting or assignment of this Lease ('Sublease Profit' or 'Assignment Proceeds').

Use of the Premises or any part thereof by any assignee, subtenant, licensee, franchisee or other occupant, other than as specifically set forth in the first sentence of Article 2, shall be permitted only if, in Landlord's reasonable judgment, the same is an upscale operation in keeping with the standards of the Building and the floor on which the Premises are located and in no event violates any of the other provisions of Article 2 or other provisions of this Lease.

Tenant shall permit Landlord, Landlord's agents and public utilities servicing the Building to erect, use and maintain concealed ducts, pipes and conduits in and through the Premises provided that such activities shall not materially diminish the size of the Premises or unreasonably interfere with Tenant's use and occupancy of the Premises. Landlord and Landlord's agents shall have the right to enter the Premises at all reasonable times, on reasonable prior notice, to examine the same, to show them to prospective purchasers, Mortgagees or lessees, and to make such repairs, alterations, improvements or additions as Landlord may deem necessary or desirable to the Premises or to any other portion of the Building.

During the one (1) year prior to the expiration of the Term, Landlord may exhibit the Premises to prospective tenants thereof. If Tenant shall not be present when for any reason entry into the Premises shall be necessary or permissible, Landlord or Landlord's agents may enter the same by a master key, and in the event of emergency, may forcibly enter the same, without rendering Landlord or such agents liable therefor (if during such entry Landlord or Landlord's agents shall accord reasonable care to Tenant's property), and without in any manner affecting this Lease.

Landlord also shall have the right at any time, without the same constituting an actual or constructive eviction and without incurring any liability to Tenant, to change the arrangement or location of entrances or passageways, doors and doorways, and corridors, elevators, stairs, toilets, or other public parts of the Building and to change the name, number or designation by which the Building is commonly known, provided such change does not unreasonably interfere with Tenant's use and occupancy of the Premises and does not change in a materially adverse way, access to or the visibility of the Premises from the exterior storefront.

Except when otherwise required by any Governmental Authority or for the health or safety of any individual, as determined by Landlord in its sole discretion, Tenant shall be permitted access to the Premises 24 hours of every day, provided that Tenant and its agents, officers, employees, invitees and business guests shall comply with the Rules and Regulations and any other security precautions which Landlord, in its sole discretion, deems necessary in connection therewith.

An Event of Default shall occur if Tenant defaults in the payment when due of any installment of Fixed Rent or any Escalation Additional Rent or other item of Rental and Tenant shall fail to remedy such default within five (5) days after Tenant shall have received notice of such default from Landlord.
An Event of Default shall occur if the Premises shall become deserted or abandoned.

An Event of Default shall occur if Tenant's interest in this Lease shall devolve upon or pass to any person or entity, whether by operation of law or otherwise, except as expressly permitted under Article 12 hereof.

An Event of Default shall occur if Tenant shall admit in writing its inability to pay its debts as they become due; or commence or institute any case, proceeding or other action seeking relief on its behalf as debtor, or to adjudicate it a bankrupt or insolvent, or seeking reorganization, arrangement, adjustment, winding-up, liquidation, dissolution, composition or other relief with respect to it or its debts under any existing or future law of any jurisdiction relating to bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization or relief of debtors; or if a trustee, receiver, or other custodian is appointed for any substantial part of the assets of Tenant which appointment is not vacated or effectively stayed within seven (7) business days.

An Event of Default shall occur if Tenant shall default in the observance or performance of any other term, covenant or condition of this Lease on Tenant's part to be observed or performed and Tenant shall fail to remedy such default within 30 days after notice by Landlord to Tenant of such default, or if such default is of such a nature that it cannot be completely remedied within said period and Tenant shall not commence within said period of 30 days all steps necessary to remedy such default.

If an Event of Default described in paragraphs A(iv), A(ii), A(iii), A(v) or A(vi) shall occur, Landlord, at any time thereafter, at its option, may give written notice to Tenant stating that this Lease and the Term shall expire and terminate on the date specified in such notice, which date shall not be less than 10 days after the giving of such notice. If on the date specified in such notice, Tenant shall have failed to cure the default which was the basis for the Event of Default, then this Lease and the Term and all rights of Tenant under this Lease shall expire and terminate.

If Tenant shall default beyond the expiration of any applicable notice and cure periods more than 3 times in any period of 6 months, or with respect to the payment of any item of Rental more than 2 times in any period of 12 months, then notwithstanding that such defaults shall have each been cured after the applicable period, any further similar default within such period shall, after notice of the same, be deemed to be deliberate and Landlord may thereafter serve a 10 days' notice of termination upon Tenant without affording Tenant an opportunity to cure such further default.

If an Event of Default described in Paragraph A(i) hereof shall occur, or this Lease shall be terminated as provided in Paragraph B(i) hereof, Landlord, without notice, may reenter and repossess the Premises using such force for that purpose as may be necessary without being liable to indictment, prosecution or damages therefor and may dispossess Tenant by summary proceedings or otherwise.

If there shall occur any Event of Default, and this Lease and the Term shall expire and come to an end as provided in Article 14: (a) Tenant shall quit and peacefully surrender the Premises to Landlord, and Landlord and its agents may immediately, or at any time after such default or after the date upon which this Lease and the Term shall expire and come to an end, re-enter the Premises or any part thereof, without notice, either by summary proceedings or by any other applicable action or proceeding, or by force, and may repossess the Premises and dispossess Tenant and any other persons from the Premises and remove any and all of their property and effects from the Premises; and (b) Landlord at Landlord's option may relet the whole or any part or parts of the Premises from time to time, either in the name of Landlord or otherwise, to such tenant or tenants, for such term or terms ending before, on or after the Expiration Date.

Tenant shall be liable for and shall pay to Landlord as damages any deficiency (the 'Deficiency') between the Rental for the period which otherwise would have constituted the unexpired portion of the Term and the net amount of rents collected under any reletting effected pursuant to the provisions of Paragraph A(i) of this Article 15 for any part of such period. The Deficiency shall be paid in monthly installments by Tenant on the days specified in this Lease for payment of installments of Fixed Rent.

If Tenant shall fail to pay any installment of Fixed Rent within ten (10) days, or shall fail to pay any Escalation Additional Rent or other item of Rental within ten (10) days after the date when such payment is due, Tenant shall pay to Landlord, in addition to such installment of Fixed Rent or other item of Rental, a sum equal to interest at the Applicable Rate on the amount unpaid, computed from the date such payment was due to and including the date of payment. In the event that any payment of Fixed Rent becomes overdue for a period in excess of five (5) days, a 'late charge' equal to five percent (5%) of the overdue payment may be charged by Landlord.

Tenant shall reimburse Landlord as additional rent, within ten (10) days after rendition of a statement, for all reasonable expenditures made by, or damages or fines sustained or incurred by, Landlord due to any default beyond the applicable notice and cure periods by Tenant under this Lease, with interest thereon at the Applicable Rate.

Landlord and Landlord's agents have made no representations or promises with respect to the Building, the Real Property or the Premises except as herein expressly set forth, and no rights, easements or licenses are acquired by Tenant by implication or otherwise except as expressly set forth herein. Landlord hereby represents and warrants to Tenant that as of the date hereof Landlord is authorized to execute this Lease.

Upon the expiration or other termination of this Lease, Tenant shall quit and surrender to Landlord the Premises, vacant, broom clean, in good order and condition, ordinary wear and tear and damage for which Tenant is not responsible under the terms of this Lease excepted, and Tenant shall remove all of its property as may be required pursuant to Article 5. If Tenant holds over after the expiration of the Term, Tenant shall pay as a use and occupancy charge for the holdover period an amount equal to the higher of (A) 1½ times the prorata Fixed Rent and Additional Rent payable during the last year of the Term without abatement, or (B) an amount equal to the then market rental value for the Premises, as established by Landlord's good faith reasonable estimate of such market rental value.

Upon Tenant paying the Fixed Rent, Escalation Additional Rent and all other items of Rental and observing and performing all the terms, covenants and conditions hereunder on Tenant's part to be observed and performed, Tenant may peaceably and quietly enjoy the Premises during the Term subject to the terms and conditions of this Lease and, as provided under Article 8 hereof, those contained in all Superior Leases and Mortgages.

Landlord and Tenant represent and warrant that Landlord and Tenant have not dealt with any broker in connection with a lease for space in the Building, and that insofar as Tenant knows no broker negotiated this Lease or is entitled to any commission in connection therewith (other than George Comfort & Sons, Inc., Esquire Properties, Inc. and Garrick-Aug Associates Store Leasing, Inc. (collectively, the 'Brokers')). Landlord shall pay any commissions owing to said Brokers for procuring and negotiating this Lease pursuant to separate agreement.

If there be any agreement between Landlord and Tenant providing for any Renewal Term, or any agreement for additional space in the Building becoming available during the Term, the right to such renewal or the delivery of a Renewal Notice prior to the expiration of the Term shall not be considered an extension thereof or a vested right in Tenant to such further term, so as to prevent Landlord from canceling this Lease during the remainder of the original Term.

No act or thing done by Landlord or Landlord's agents during the Term shall be deemed an acceptance of a surrender of the Premises, and no agreement to accept such surrender shall be valid unless in writing signed by Landlord. No employee of Landlord or of Landlord's agents shall have any power to accept the keys of the Premises prior to the termination of this Lease.

The failure of Landlord to seek redress for violation of, or to insist upon the strict performance of, any covenant or condition of this Lease, or any of the Rules and Regulations set forth or hereafter adopted by Landlord, shall not prevent a subsequent act, which would have originally constituted a violation, from having all of the force and effect of an original violation. No provision of this Lease shall be deemed to have been waived by Landlord unless such waiver be in writing signed by Landlord.

The respective parties hereto shall and they hereby do waive trial by jury in any action, proceeding or counterclaim brought by either of the parties hereto against the other (except for personal injury or property damage) on any matters whatsoever arising out of or in any way connected with this Lease, the relationship of Landlord and Tenant, Tenant's use or occupancy of the Premises, or for the enforcement of any remedy under any statute, emergency or otherwise.

This Lease shall be governed by and construed and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of New York. Tenant irrevocably consents and agrees that any legal action, suit or proceeding arising out of or in any way connected with this Lease may be instituted or brought in the courts of the State of New York, in the County of New York, or the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York, and by execution and delivery of this Lease, Tenant irrevocably accepts and submits, for itself and in respect of its property, generally and unconditionally, to the exclusive jurisdiction of either of such courts.

This Lease and the obligation of Tenant to pay Rental hereunder and perform all of the other covenants and agreements hereunder on the part of Tenant shall not be affected, impaired or excused because Landlord is unable to fulfill any of its obligations under this Lease if Landlord is prevented or delayed from doing so by reason of strikes, labor troubles, accident, adjustments of insurance, or any cause beyond Landlord's reasonable control ('Unavoidable Delays'). Nothing hereinbefore contained shall permit or be deemed to permit Tenant from being excused from the payment of Rental or other amounts of money.

Tenant shall not do or permit any act or thing to be done upon the Premises which may subject Landlord to any liability or responsibility for injury, damages to persons or property or to any liability by reason of any violation of law or of any Requirement, but shall exercise such control over the Premises as to fully protect Landlord against such liability. Tenant shall indemnify and save the Indemnities harmless from and against (i) all claims arising from any act, omission or negligence of Tenant, its contractors, licensees, agents, servants, employees, subtenants, invitees or visitors, (ii) all claims arising from any accident, injury or damage in the Premises, (iii) all claims arising from any accident, injury or damage occurring outside of the Premises but anywhere within or about the Real Property where such accident, injury or damage results from an act, omission or negligence of Tenant or Tenant's agents, and (iv) any breach, violation or non-performance of any covenant, condition or agreement in this Lease set forth and contained on the part of Tenant to be fulfilled, kept, observed and performed.

If any claim, action or proceeding is made or brought against Landlord, which claim, action or proceeding Tenant shall be obligated to indemnify Landlord against pursuant to the terms of this Lease, then, upon demand by Landlord, Tenant, at its sole cost and expense, shall resist or defend such claim, action or proceeding in the Landlord's name, if necessary, by such attorneys as Landlord shall approve. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if any claim, action or proceeding involves a potential liability of Five Million Dollars ($5,000,000) or more Landlord may retain its own attorneys to defend or assist in defending, and Tenant shall pay the fees and disbursements of such attorneys if Landlord determines that the attorneys retained by Tenant (or its insurance company) are not reasonably acceptable to Landlord.

All bills, statements, consents, notices, demands, requests or other communications given or required to be given under this Lease shall be in writing. Any notice of default shall be deemed sufficiently given or rendered if sent by registered or certified mail (return receipt requested) or hand delivered, provided a signed receipt is obtained, or by overnight delivery service providing for written evidence of delivery, addressed: (A) to Tenant at the address first above written, Attention: Real Estate Department, with a copy to Westerman Ball Ederer Miller and Sharfstein, LLP, 170 Old Country Road, Mineola, New York 11501; and (B) to Landlord at Landlord's address set forth in this Lease, with a copy to Stuart D. Byron, Esq., c/o Blank Rome Tenzer Greenblatt LLP, 405 Lexington Avenue, New York, New York 10174.

Landlord shall provide heating for the ground floor portion of the Premises as required for Tenant's comfortable use and normal occupancy from 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., Mondays through Fridays and from 9:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. on Saturdays. Tenant shall be responsible for the maintenance and repair, at its sole cost and expense, of any air-conditioning system currently existing or which Tenant may hereafter install in the Premises. Landlord, throughout the Term, shall have free access to any and all mechanical installations of Landlord. Tenant shall not construct partitions or other obstructions which may interfere with Landlord's free access thereto, or interfere with the moving of Landlord's equipment to and from the enclosures containing said installations.

Tenant, at its expense, shall cause the Premises to be cleaned and exterminated against infestation by vermin, rodents or roaches regularly and whenever there shall be evidence of any infestation. All cleaning of the Premises done by Tenant shall be performed in a manner reasonably satisfactory to Landlord. Prior to opening the Premises for business, Tenant shall construct and maintain an area for the temporary storage of garbage pending its removal from the Premises so as to avoid any noxious or offensive odors escaping therefrom. Such temporary area is to be cleaned and disinfected daily and exterminated not less than twice per month. All garbage that is retained therein shall be in covered receptacles. Tenant shall, throughout the Term hereof, dispose of all garbage, refuse and waste arising from the conduct of its business in the Premises, daily, and shall enter into a service contract for the regular disposal of all such garbage, refuse and waste.

Landlord at its sole cost may elect to install a water meter in the Premises to measure Tenant's water consumption. In such event, Tenant shall keep said meter in good working order and repair at Tenant's own cost and expense. Tenant shall pay for water consumed as shown on said meter, within 20 days after bills are rendered as additional rent. Tenant shall also pay the sewer rent or any other tax, rent, levy or charge assessed, imposed or that shall become a lien upon the Premises or the Real Property pursuant to any Requirement made or issued in connection with any such metered use, consumption, maintenance or supply of water, water system, or sewage or sewage connection or system. The bill rendered by Landlord for the above shall be based upon Tenant's consumption and shall be payable by Tenant as additional rent within twenty (20) days of rendition.

Landlord reserves the right to stop service of the HVAC System or the electrical, plumbing or other mechanical systems or facilities in the Building when necessary, by reason of accident or emergency, or for repairs, additions, alterations, replacements or improvements in the reasonable judgment of Landlord desirable or necessary to be made, until said repairs, alterations, replacements or improvements shall have been completed. Landlord shall have no responsibility or liability for interruption, curtailment or failure to supply HVAC or plumbing when prevented by Unavoidable Delays or by any Requirement of any Governmental Authority or due to the exercise of its right to stop service as provided in this Paragraph D.

Tenant shall not permit any food or noxious odors to emanate from the Premises to an unreasonable extent in Landlord's reasonable judgment. Tenant shall make all reasonable efforts to minimize the emanation of such food or noxious odors from the Premises. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Tenant installs a 'kitchen' in the Premises, then Tenant shall install a ventilation system in the Premises venting to 37th Street (subject to Landlord's prior written approval which will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed) at Tenant's sole cost and expense. For the purposes of this Lease, the term 'kitchen' shall mean the installation of a range with burners for cooking and/or one or more ovens for cooking and shall not include the use of microwave ovens for warming previously cooked food.
Tenant understands and agrees that Landlord will have no obligation to supply heat, water or other utilities or facilities therefor to the basement portion of the Premises.

Either party may request arbitration of any matter in dispute with respect to Landlord's approval with respect to an Alteration or Landlord's approval of a request by Tenant to assign and/or sublet this Lease. The party requesting arbitration shall do so by giving notice to that effect to the other party, and both parties shall promptly thereafter jointly apply to the American Arbitration Association (or any successor organization thereto) in the City and County of New York for the appointment of a single arbitrator. The arbitration shall be conducted in accordance with the then prevailing rules of the American Arbitration Association in the City and County of New York applicable to the dispute in question.

If an excavation shall be made upon land adjacent to the Premises or the Building, or shall be authorized to be made, Tenant shall afford to the person causing or authorized to cause such excavation, license to enter upon the Premises for the purpose of doing such work as said person shall deem necessary to preserve the wall or the Building from injury or damage and to support the same by proper foundations without any claim for damages or indemnity against Landlord or diminution or abatement of Fixed Rent, Escalation Additional Rent or other item of Rental, provided however that if any such excavation shall totally block access to the Premises and thereby prevent Tenant from opening for business for more than ten (10) consecutive days, the Rental shall be abated until access to the Premises shall be restored.

Landlord shall have the right, exercisable at least once in each year during the Term, to close to public use, by means of a rope, chain or other effective device, for a period of at least twelve (12) consecutive hours between sunrise and sunset, a portion of the Real Property or the Building to prevent public dedication thereof, and may prominently display a sign or notice that such area is closed to public use. Landlord shall in no event be liable to Tenant for any loss or damages Tenant may suffer during such periods, nor shall Tenant be entitled to any diminution or abatement of Fixed Rent, Escalation Additional Rent or other item of Rental due to any inconvenience or annoyance suffered by reason thereof.

This Lease is offered for signature by Tenant and it is understood that this Lease shall not be binding upon Landlord or Tenant unless and until Landlord and Tenant shall have executed and delivered to the other a fully executed copy of this Lease.

The obligations of Landlord under this Lease shall not be binding upon Landlord named herein after the sale, conveyance, assignment or transfer by such Landlord of its interest in the Building or the Real Property, as the case may be, and in the event of any such sale, conveyance, assignment or transfer, Landlord shall be and hereby is entirely freed and relieved of all covenants and obligations of Landlord hereunder, provided such transferee assumes the obligations of Landlord hereunder. The liability of Landlord for Landlord's obligations under this Lease shall be limited to Landlord's interest in the Real Property.

All references in this Lease to the consent or approval of Landlord shall be deemed to mean the written consent or approval of Landlord and no consent or approval of Landlord shall be effective for any purpose unless such consent or approval is set forth in a written instrument executed by Landlord. Wherever it shall be specifically provided in this Lease that the consent of a party hereto shall not be unreasonably withheld, a response to a request for such consent shall not be unreasonably delayed.

If any of the provisions of this Lease or the application thereof to any person or circumstance shall be found invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of this Lease, or the application of such provision to persons or circumstances other than those as to whom or which it is held invalid or unenforceable, shall not be affected thereby, and every provision of this Lease shall be valid and enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by law.

This Lease contains the entire agreement between the parties and all prior negotiations and agreements are merged herein. Any executory agreement hereafter made shall be ineffective to change, modify, discharge or effect an abandonment of this Lease in whole or in part unless such executory agreement is in writing and signed by the party against whom enforcement of the change, modification, discharge or abandonment is sought.

So long as Tenant shall not be in default beyond applicable cure periods, if any, under this Lease and shall be occupying the entire Premises for the conduct of business as a 'Cosi' restaurant, Landlord agrees not to enter into a lease for a store on the ground floor fronting on Seventh Avenue of the Building for the primary purpose of (i) the sale of coffee or espresso based beverages, or (ii) a casual restaurant or sandwich shop during the Term.

Tenant shall submit detailed architectural design drawings indicating the materials, location, installation and characters of any exterior or interior window signage to Landlord for Landlord's approval, which approval Landlord may withhold in its sole and reasonable discretion. After Landlord has approved in writing the design, location, placement, installation and characters of a sign (an 'Approved Sign'), such Approved Sign (i) shall be fabricated by signmakers selected by Tenant, (ii) shall be installed in a manner reasonably approved by Landlord, (iii) shall be maintained by Tenant in good condition and repair, and (iv) shall not be altered without Landlord's prior written approval.

Other than an Approved Sign, Tenant shall not, without the prior consent of Landlord (which Landlord may withhold in its sole discretion), place, maintain, exhibit, inscribe, paint or affix any other sign, insignia, advertisement, lettering, notice, poster, photograph, gate or other object on any part of the outside of the Premises, the Building, the corridor walls, the glass of any window or door or within five (5) feet of the inner side of the glass of any window or door, or in any area which would be conspicuously visible from the surrounding street and sidewalks, and sidewalks or from portions of the Building not included within the Premises.

In the event Tenant violates any of the provisions of this Article 29, Landlord, with or without notice to Tenant, may remove the offending sign and/or restore the external appearance of the Premises and make such repairs to the immediately surrounding area as Landlord deems appropriate without any liability to itself. Tenant shall reimburse Landlord for any and all expenses incurred in connection with such removal and repair on demand.

If Landlord is unable to give possession of the Premises on the date of the commencement of the term hereof because of the holding-over or retention of possession of any tenant, undertenant or occupants or for any other reason, Landlord shall not be subject to any liability for failure to give possession on said date and the validity of the Lease shall not be impaired under such circumstances. The Commencement Date shall be postponed and the rent payable hereunder shall be abated (provided Tenant is not responsible for Landlord's inability to obtain possession) until after Landlord shall have given Tenant written notice that the Premises are available for Tenant's occupancy.

In the event that Landlord fails to Substantially Complete item 2 (and item 3 subject to Article 1(B)(iii) hereof) of Landlord's Work as set forth on Schedule A on or before sixty (60) days after the Commencement Date (subject to the terms of this Lease) other than as a result of or arising from the fault, act or omission of Tenant or its contractors, and such failure is the sole cause of Tenant not being able to open for the ordinary conduct of its business and notifies Landlord thereof, Tenant shall be entitled to a rent credit equal to one (1) day for each day commencing with the sixty-first (61st) day after the Commencement Date until Landlord so Substantially Completes such item 2 or Tenant is able to open for the ordinary conduct of its business or Tenant in fact opens for business, whichever shall first occur.

In the event the Commencement Date has not occurred within one (1) year of the date of this Lease other than as a result of the fault of Tenant, Tenant shall have the right to cancel this Lease by giving Landlord written notice within ten (10) days after the anniversary of the date of this Lease, whereupon the parties shall have no further obligations to each other.

Notwithstanding any other provision of this Lease, Tenant shall schedule and arrange for the removal of all trash, garbage and refuse of any kind at Tenant's sole cost and expense so that removal is made between 7:00 p.m. and 8:00 a.m. the following day, and, in any event, daily on business days and on Saturdays and Sundays if Tenant shall be open for business on either of such days.

Tenant shall provide, operate and maintain a separate storage area within the Premises for the storage of all garbage, trash and refuse for the purpose of containing noxious odors and preventing health hazards following ten (10) days notice from Landlord, if Landlord in its reasonable discretion shall determine that same is necessary. Landlord shall make available to Tenant on a temporary, revocable basis a location in the loading dock for Tenant to place a bin for the storage of its trash.
Tenant shall plan such storage area so that all operations related thereto shall not be able to be viewed by business patrons of the Premises or by pedestrians from the street level.

Tenant shall do all things necessary to keep the Premises reasonably clean at all times, including, without limitation, cleaning all floors, walls, furniture, furnishings and equipment, it being understood that the performance of such functions by the Tenant shall be done in such a manner as to adhere to a high standard of cleanliness and attractiveness.
Tenant shall dispose of garbage and waste from the Premises in such a manner as not to permit any overflow in sewer or sewer lines used by Tenant.
Tenant shall store all garbage in the Premises or in any other area made available to Tenant until the scheduled hour for the removal thereof.

Tenant covenants and agrees that at all times (i) the business to be conducted at, through and from the Premises and the kind and quality of the merchandise, food and services offered in the conduct thereof will be first-class and in accordance with the requirements of Articles 2 and 12, (ii) the sales methods employed in said business, as well as all other elements of merchandising, display and advertising, will be dignified and in conformity with the standards of practice obtaining among superior type 'upscale' casual restaurants, and (iii) the appearance of the Premises (including the lighting and other appurtenances thereto), the appearance, number, location, nature and subject matter of all displays and exhibits placed or installed in or about the Premises, will only be such as meet with the Landlord's approval (to be exercised in its reasonable discretion unless conspicuously visible from outside of the Premises, in which case same shall be subject to Landlord's sole and absolute discretion).

Tenant further covenants and agrees that Tenant shall, at its sole cost and expense, keep the Premises clean, and in a neat, sanitary condition, keep the ventilating hoods over ranges and cooking equipment and duct work, if any, clean, keep all plumbing in the Premises and sanitary systems and installations serving the Premises in a good state of repair and operating condition, and keep the sidewalks, and plaza areas adjacent to the Premises clean and free from any snow, garbage or debris.

Tenant shall operate Tenant's business in the Premises throughout the Term of this Lease and keep the Premises open for business at least between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. on all days other than Saturdays and Sundays, legal holidays and days upon which Landlord permits the Premises to be closed and in any event for at least fifty (50) hours each week. Tenant's covenant to keep the Premises open for business during Minimum Retail Hours shall be subject to temporary closings or discontinuance of operations (a) to permit the repair of any damage to the Premises, (b) to undertake any alteration or refurbishing of the Premises, (c) due to a taking by eminent domain, or (d) due to strikes (each a 'Permitted Closure'), but in no event shall such Permitted Closure continue for a period in excess of thirty (30) days, subject to force majeure, but in no event to exceed ninety (90) days.

Tenant shall keep the fixtures, furnishings, decorations and equipment in the Premises in good order and repair so as to preserve the good appearance of the Premises consistent with the general first-class, high-quality standard for the Building.

Tenant shall not install, place or permit any awning, antenna or other projection on the outside walls of the Premises unless previously consented to in writing by the Landlord (which Landlord may withhold in its sole discretion) and each such awning, antenna or other projection so consented to shall, to the reasonable satisfaction of the Landlord, be kept clean and in good order and state of repair and appearance by and at the sole cost and expense of Tenant.

Tenant shall not use, play or operate or permit to be used, played or operated any sound making or sound reproducing device in such manner and under such conditions so that sound shall be heard outside of the Premises, and Tenant covenants and agrees that Tenant, at its sole cost and expense, shall observe, comply with and adopt such means and precautions as Landlord may from time to time request in such connection.
Tenant shall not permit Tenant's employees or customers to congregate or to loiter in the Building's fire stairway or other public areas of or adjacent to the Building.
